title: Hides, Skins And Leather Trade (Duties and Powers of Inspectors) Regulations, 2010
title_en: Hides, Skins And Leather Trade (Duties and Powers of Inspectors) Regulations, 2010

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Hides, Skin and leather trade (Duties and powers of Inspectors) Regulations, 2010.

provisions.title: Applica­tion
provisions.content: These Regulations shall apply to Inspectors appointed under the Act in their areas of jurisdiction.

provisions.title: Interpre­tation
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context otherwise requires:— “ Act ” means the Hides, Skins, and Leather Trade Act; “ Director ” means the Director responsible for hides, skins and leather development in the Ministry responsible for Livestock Development; “ Inspector ” means a ‘person appointed to be an inspector under section 28 of the act; “ Inspector Identification Card ” means the Identification card, issued by the inspector under regulation 6 ; and. “ Minister ” means the Minister responsible for livestock;

provisions.title: Objective
provisions.content: The objective of these regulations is to promote production of quality hides, skins, or leather. Part II – Appointment, duties, and powers of inspectors

provisions.title: Appoint­ment of inspec­tors
provisions.content: (1) The Director may appoint by notice published in the Gazette persons from amongst registered veterinarians, Livestock Officers or Livestock Field officers to be inspectors for every local Government Authority. (2) These inspector shall have adequate knowledge of grading hides, skins, and leather.

provisions.title: Issuance of identifi­cation cards
provisions.content: (1) The Director shall, immediately after the appointment of inspectors under regulation 5 cause, to be issued an identification card for each inspector. (2) The identification card issued under sub-regulation (1) shall be in the form prescribed in the schedule to these regulations and shall contain:— (a) identification card number; (b) the full name of the holder; (c) designation of the holder; (d) the area of operation; (e) designation of appointing authority; (f) date of issue; (g) photograph of the holder; and (h) such other terms and conditions as may be prescribed on the identification card;

provisions.title: Powers and duties of Inspec­tors or police
provisions.content: Powers and duties of Inspector or police shall be as prescribed under section 20 (2), 22 (3) and 29 (2) of the Act .

provisions.title: Tenure of office of Inspec­tors
provisions.content: (1) any inspector appointed under these regulations shall hold office for a period to be determined by the appointing authority. (2) Notwithstanding to sub-regulation (1) , the Director may at any time terminate an inspector if he fails without good cause to:— (a) perform his duties; or (b) observe any of the conditions attached to his appointment.

provisions.title: Surrender of identifica­tion card
provisions.content: (1) Any inspector, who has been terminated, retired or resigned from public service shall within fourteen days from the date of his termination, retirement, or resignation surrender the identification card issued to him to the Director or authorized officer in the Local Government Authority. (2) Subject to provisions of sub-regulation (1) an inspector who fails to surrender the identification card within the period specified under sub-regulation (1) commits an offence and shall be liable to a fine not less than fifty thousand Tanzanian shillings or to imprisonment for a term not less than one month or to both. Part III – Offences and penalties

provisions.title: Offences and penalties
provisions.content: (1) Any person who:— (a) willfully delays or obstructs any inspector or police in the exercise of his powers and duties under these regulations; or (b) refuses or fails without reasonable cause to give any information which he is lawfully required to give under these regulations; and (c) gives any information, which is false in a material particular by which he reasonably knows to be untrue, commits an offence and shall upon conviction be liable to a fine not exceeding one million Tanzanian shillings or imprisonment for a term not exceeding twelve months or to both.
